Text: Kevin is a creative dynamo and everyone should keep their eyes peeled.  In a field that's always pulling toward neutered, "safe" content (sorry, tech marketers), he 100% gets that cutting through the sad, grey milk of today's internet is Job #1.   Not only that, he delivers. A power metal song about Azure SSO. A spy miniseries that conveniently brings your product differentiators to life. A teardown of the latest world-breaking AI model (released a couple of hours after it drops and bringing 13,000+ organic views with it).  He's got the mind to hone a strategy, the taste to make it good, and the chops to make it happen. He's also a joy to chat with every day.   Go hire him now before someone else does.

Neil immediately brightened the Wenco office with his enthusiasm and cheeriness when he joined in 2019. During his time in the marketing department, he spearheaded efforts to improve Wenco's employer brand and extend the company's social media presence to customers in a challenging and traditional industry. He was instrumental to key digital media milestones and I unreservedly endorse his work for any organization looking to cut through the clutter in today's digital landscape and gain a stronger foothold online.

I worked with Loretta as a fellow writer at Wenco (except based in North America and in the marketing department). During that time, we collaborated on key projects related to company documentation processes, writing style, and consistency. Loretta was always helpful and eager to share her rich experience and understanding of good technical documentation. Deeply knowledgeable and a pleasure to work with, I think she'd be an asset to any organization and I'd welcome the opportunity to work with her again.

Mark Cluett was a consummate professional as a manager, maintaining a strong open-door policy. During my time working with him, he demonstrated the highest quality of work and ethics. I wholeheartedly endorse him in any future pursuits for those looking for an honest, driven, and creative colleague.    Sincerely,    Devon Wells

To Whom It May Concern,    I wish to express a sincere endorsement of Wiley Skewes as an employee and coworker for any future employment.     During our time together at Shane English School, a small private language school in Dalian, China, I found Wiley to exhibit a strong dedication to his work and craft. He consistently prepared his work in a detailed fashion and strove for creative approaches to assigned tasks, frequently inspiring innovation in the rest of his fellow staff. His enthusiasm, spirit, and desire for excellence proved a welcome asset to our company.    I recommend Wiley for anyone seeking a passionate, motivated, or innovative member to add to their team. Please feel free to contact me for any further information.    Sincerely,    Devon Wells
